a:r.a:t: Private And Confidential | Photon® Interactive UK Limited, Copyright ©2026
Queries the knowledge base, Remedy history, and runbooks for similar incidents. Surfaces matching resolution patterns and cross-references with current system state.
Calculates incident priority (P1–P4), business impact score, and severity classification. Determines whether the super-agent HITL gate should be activated.
Synthesises telemetry, knowledge results, and priority data into a structured Root Cause Analysis summary, ready for human review or direct downstream action.
Continuously executes synthetic user journeys across every critical Darden flow — proactively catching degradation before real customers are impacted.
Replays the failed journey to confirm and characterize the failure, distinguishing real degradations from transient blips and reducing false-positive escalations.
Pulls telemetry from across the Darden stack to correlate the failure with infrastructure signals, building a precise timeline of contributing factors.
Auto-creates a Remedy ticket with root cause, affected components, timeline, and recommended resolution steps. Publishes full RCA findings to the knowledge base.
Posts structured incident updates to the relevant Microsoft Teams channels and sends email notifications to stakeholder groups with full incident context and ticket reference.
For P1/P2 incidents, triggers an xMatters page to the on-call engineer. Super-agent activates the HITL gate — pausing automation until a human approves or overrides the response plan.
10 specialized AI agents · Orchestrated by Journey Sentinel Super Agent · HITL gate on P1/P2 · Knowledge base grows with every incident
After resolution, publishes updated runbooks and enriched incident patterns to the knowledge base. Feeds improved signals back into Step 1 — the system gets smarter with every incident.
